content.full_text:
MAR 31 '92 15:55     CITY OF PACIFICA     833 F01

Susan M. Schechtman
City Attorney
(415)738-7307
FAX (415) 359-6038

Office of the City Attorney
City of Pacifica
170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, California 94044

March 31, 1992

BY FACSIMILE

URGENT RESPONSE NEEDED BY APRIL 13, 1992


John W. Wallace, Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
FAX: (916) 327-2026

Re: Request for Advice Letter -

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I have been authorized by Councilmembers John Schneider and Bonnie Wells of the City Council of the City of Pacifica to request an advice letter from the FPPC on their behalf. A response is urgently needed by April 13th. The advice letter concerns whether Government Code § [illegible] will permit Councilmembers Schneider and Wells to participate in actions scheduled to be heard that day under the rule of "legally required participation".

BACKGROUND FACTS

The Pacifica City Council consists of five members. A quorum of the City Council requires three members. Decisions of the City Council typically require a majority of the quorum. That is, most matters can be passed on a 2-1 or 2-0 vote. However, certain matters by statute require three affirmative votes. These include orders for the payment of money and adoption of ordinances. See Government Code § 36936.

On March 10, 1992, four of the five Pacifica City Councilmembers were recalled in a special recall election. Pursuant to Elections Code § 27334, all Councilmembers retain their seats until the successor election currently scheduled to be held on June 2, 1992.

However, two members of the Council immediately resigned. Therefore, there are only three members of the Pacifica City Council remaining in office. Two seats are vacant. The remaining

MAR 31 '92 15:55        CITY OF PACIFICA                    833 P02

,

,

Councilmembers consist of Councilmembers Schneider, Wells and
Vasey.

In the event one member is disqualified due to a conflict of
interest under Government Code § 87100, the City Council will lack
a quorum and will be unable to take any action other than to
adjourn.

To avoid matters involving conflicts of interest, the Council
is deferring action on as many matters as possible until after the
vacant seats are filled at the successor election on June 2, 1992.

However, the City is legally required to take action on two
matters prior to June 2, 1992. In these matters, Councilmembers
Schneider and Wells would be prohibited from making or
participating in such decisions pursuant to the Political Reform
Act and therefore the Council would be unable to act. Therefore,
Councilmembers Schneider and Wells have authorized me to ask
whether they may participate under the "legally required"
provisions of Government Code § 87101.

COUNCILMEMBER SCHNEIDER

The pending decision is as follows: McKellar Development
Corporation of Pacifica ("McKellar") has filed a subdivision
application with the City of Pacifica to subdivide an existing
hotel. McKellar has retained the services of Councilmember
Schneider as a consultant concerning various aspects of the
subdivision development proposal for which he has received in excess
of $250 in the past twenty-four months. Furthermore, it is
reasonably foreseeable that, in the event the subdivision were
approved, Councilmember Schneider would receive material financial
gain in acting as a real estate agent for sale of the subdivided
units. Councilmember Schneider has acknowledged and agrees that he
has a material financial interest in the City's decision concerning
this subdivision application, that the decision will have a
material financial effect on a source of income to him, and that he
would normally be prohibited from participating in such a decision.

On March 16, 1992, the Pacifica Planning Commission denied the
subdivision application. On March 25, 1992, McKellar filed an
appeal of that decision to the City Council. Government Code §
6452.5(a) requires that such an appeal hearing be held within
thirty days of the date of the filing of the appeal. Therefore,
this matter cannot be held after June 2, 1992. The next regularly
scheduled Council meeting is April 13th, and the matter has been
scheduled to be heard on that date. Accordingly, it is imperative
to know whether or not Councilmember Schneider can participate if
he cannot be disqualified, the City will lack a quorum and
cannot legally take action on the matter. The Pacifica City
Council is the only body authorized to hear appeals of subdivision
applications. There is no other alternative source of decision
making authorized by law in the event Councilmember Schneider were
disqualified.

MAR 31 '92 15:56         CITY OF PACIFICA                    833 P03

.

    Based on the foregoing facts, the specific questions we pose
are as follows:

    1.   Under the foregoing facts, is Councilmember Schneider
permitted to participate in hearing appeal of the
McKellar subdivision application on April 13th pursuant
to Government Code § 87101?

    2.   If so, what restrictions on his participation should be
followed?  That is, is he required to remain silent or
can he participate in debate? Further, is he required to
abstain or is he permitted to vote yes or no?  In other
words, should he sit silently at the dais and be counted
solely in order to form a quorum, or can he actually
discuss and vote on the matter?

                         COUNCILMEMBER WELLS

    The background facts are the same.

    The specific facts are as follows:  Councilmember Wells is an
employee of Pacifica Gas & Electric Company ("PG&E").  The City of
Pacifica is a utility customer of PG&E.  Pursuant to the O'Shea
Advice Letter, No. I-83-331, and the enclosed letter from the FPPC
Legal Division, I have advised Ms. Wells to disqualify herself from
voting on the City's monthly payments for PG&E's utility bills.
She has always done so.  However, if she does so on April 13, 1992,
the Council will lack a quorum.  Further, the council will lack the
three affirmative votes required to approve payments of money as
required by Government Code § 36936.  There is no local ordinance
which authorizes an alternative source of decision making.  The
payment of the PG&E bills has already been deferred once and can
not properly be stayed until after June 2, 1992.

    Accordingly, we pose the same two questions regarding Ms.
Wells as we posed above concerning Councilmember Schneider.

    Please let me know if you need any other facts or documents in
order to prepare your response.  I will fax those documents to you
immediately.  I apologize for the need to request an expedited
response.  However, events have occurred very rapidly since March
10, 1992.  I'd be happy to offer any assistance I can provide.

                                    Very truly yours,

                                    [signature]
                                    SUSAN M. SCHECTMAN
                                    City Attorney

SMS:fd
cc:  City Council

Enclosures

sections.facts:
FACTS

The Pacifica City Council consists of five members. A quorum of the City Council requires three members. Decisions of the City Council typically require a majority of the quorum. That is, most matters can be passed on a 2-1 or 2-0 vote. However, certain matters by statute require three affirmative votes. These include orders for the payment of money and adoption of ordinances. See Government Code § 36936.

On March 10, 1992, four of the five Pacifica City Councilmembers were recalled in a special recall election. Pursuant to Elections Code § 27334, all Councilmembers retain their seats until the successor election currently scheduled to be held on June 2, 1992.

However, two members of the Council immediately resigned. Therefore, there are only three members of the Pacifica City Council remaining in office. Two seats are vacant. The remaining

Councilmembers consist of Councilmembers Schneider, Wells and Vasey.

In the event one member is disqualified due to a conflict of interest under Government Code § 87100, the City Council will lack a quorum and will be unable to take any action other than to adjourn.

To avoid matters involving conflicts of interest, the Council is deferring action on as many matters as possible until after the vacant seats are filled at the successor election on June 2, 1992.

However, the City is legally required to take action on two matters prior to June 2, 1992. In these matters, Councilmembers Schneider and Wells would be prohibited from making or participating in such decisions pursuant to the Political Reform Act and therefore the Council would be unable to act. Therefore, Councilmembers Schneider and Wells have authorized me to ask whether they may participate under the "legally required" provisions of Government Code § 87101.

COUNCILMEMBER SCHNEIDER

The pending decision is as follows: McKellar Development Corporation of Pacifica ("McKellar") has filed a subdivision application with the City of Pacifica to subdivide an existing hotel. McKellar has retained the services of Councilmember Schneider as a consultant concerning various aspects of the subdivision development proposal for which he has received in excess of $250 in the last twelve months. Further, it is reasonably foreseeable that, in the event the subdivision were approved, Councilmember Schneider would receive material financial gain in acting as a real estate agent for sale of the subdivided units. Councilmember Schneider has acknowledged and agrees that he has a material financial interest in the City's decision concerning this subdivision application, that the decision will have a material financial effect on a source of income to him, and that he would normally be prohibited from participating in such a decision.

On March 16, 1992, the Pacifica Planning Commission denied the subdivision application. On March 25, 1992, McKellar filed an appeal of that decision to the City Council. Government Code § 66452.5(a) requires that such an appeal hearing be held within thirty days of the date of the filing of the appeal. Therefore, this matter cannot be held after June 2, 1992. The next regularly scheduled Council meeting is April 13th, and the matter has been scheduled to be heard on that date. Accordingly, it is imperative to know whether or not Councilmember Schneider can participate. If he cannot and is disqualified, the City will lack a quorum and cannot legally take action on the matter. The Pacifica City Council is the only body authorized to hear appeals of subdivision applications. There is no other alternative source of decision making authorized by law in the event Councilmember Schneider were disqualified.

Based on the foregoing facts, the specific questions we pose are as follows:

1. Under the foregoing facts, is Councilmember Schneider permitted to participate in hearing the appeal of the McKellar subdivision application on April 13th pursuant to Government Code § 87101?

2. If so, what restrictions on his participation should be followed? That is, is he required to remain silent or can he participate in debate? Further, is he required to abstain or is he permitted to vote yes or no? In other words, should he sit silently at the dais and be counted solely in order to form a quorum, or can he actually discuss and vote on the matter?

COUNCILMEMBER WELLS

The background facts are the same.

The specific facts are as follows: Councilmember Wells is an employee of Pacifica Gas & Electric Company ("PG&E"). The City of Pacifica is a utility customer of PG&E. Pursuant to the O'Shea Advice Letter, No. I-88-332, and the enclosed letter from the FPPC Legal Division, I have advised Ms. Wells to disqualify herself from voting on the City's monthly payments for PG&E's utility bills. She has always done so. However, if she does so on April 13, 1992, the Council will lack a quorum. Further, the Council will lack the three affirmative votes required to approve payments of money as required by Government Code § 36936. There is no local ordinance which authorizes an alternative source of decision making. The payment of the PG&E bills has already been deferred once and cannot properly be stayed until after June 2, 1992.

Accordingly, we pose the same two questions regarding Ms. Wells as we posed above concerning Councilmember Schneider.

Please let me know if you need any other facts or documents in order to prepare your response. I will fax those documents to you immediately. I apologize for the need to request an expedited response. However, events have occurred very rapidly since March 10, 1992. I'd be happy to offer any assistance I can provide.
